content.text:
[
  {
    "author": "natolambert",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "A big problem with this is that we don't really have a clear description of what mythos capabilities are. \n\nA model on each of the benchmarks in the launch blog post, sure. \n\nA model that you can swap right in for the same use-cases and notice no drop in perf? Doubt it.",
    "full_post": "A big problem with this is that we don't really have a clear description of what mythos capabilities are. \n\nA model on each of the benchmarks in the launch blog post, sure. \n\nA model that you can swap right in for the same use-cases and notice no drop in perf? Doubt it.\n\nQuoted: Dario seems to think China and open source will hit mythos capabilities in 6-12 months",
    "url": "https://x.com/natolambert/status/2045573304518590567",
    "key_claims": ["Lack of clear definition for Mythos capabilities", "Doubts open source models will match closed models in practical use-cases soon"]
  },
  {
    "author": "natolambert",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "I’m feeling more optimistic we can do this. Beginning of a new chapter here come end of the year.",
    "full_post": "I’m feeling more optimistic we can do this. Beginning of a new chapter here come end of the year.\n\nReplied to: America leading in open source/open weight AI is crucial. this matters at all levels of the stack from models to harnesses to applications.",
    "url": "https://x.com/natolambert/status/2045551535535555024",
    "key_claims": ["Optimism about US leading in open source AI", "New chapter in open AI by end of year"]
  },
  {
    "author": "simonw",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-19",
    "text": "Since Anthropic publish their system prompts we can generate a diff between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7 - here are my notes on what's changed https://simonwillison.net/2026/Apr/18/opus-system-prompt/",
    "full_post": "Since Anthropic publish their system prompts we can generate a diff between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7 - here are my notes on what's changed https://simonwillison.net/2026/Apr/18/opus-system-prompt/",
    "url": "https://x.com/simonw/status/2045655052548022690",
    "key_claims": ["Changes in Claude Opus system prompts from 4.6 to 4.7"]
  },
  {
    "author": "simonw",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "I'd rather that than type hundreds of lines of code by hand",
    "full_post": "I'd rather that than type hundreds of lines of code by hand\n\nReplied to: I cant understand why people feel like it is a nice experience as a programmer to have to review randomly generated code that solves a specific task.",
    "url": "https://x.com/simonw/status/2045457390754206151",
    "key_claims": ["Preference for AI-generated code over manual coding for large amounts"]
  },
  {
    "author": "simonw",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "Step one is to have good automated tests - then approach every p by researching, planning, implementing with red/green TDD, then validating with both human-manual and coding-agent-manual testing",
    "full_post": "Step one is to have good automated tests - then approach every p by researching, planning, implementing with red/green TDD, then validating with both human-manual and coding-agent-manual testing\n\nReplied to: How do you use it on brownfield code? What’s the method?",
    "url": "https://x.com/simonw/status/2045410739687747772",
    "key_claims": ["Use automated tests and TDD with coding agents for brownfield code"]
  },
  {
    "author": "simonw",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "\"English (nor any other natural language) is not a safe way to specify anything that needs well-defined, certain, reliable outcomes.\" - right, that's why I use coding agents to accelerate the process of building code in unambiguous programming languages which I then review",
    "full_post": "\"English (nor any other natural language) is not a safe way to specify anything that needs well-defined, certain, reliable outcomes.\" - right, that's why I use coding agents to accelerate the process of building code in unambiguous programming languages which I then review\n\nQuoted: My beliefs have not changed: agentic AI is not the way forward for SWE",
    "url": "https://x.com/simonw/status/2045370523656560780",
    "key_claims": ["Coding agents accelerate building reliable code", "Natural language insufficient for precise specifications"]
  },
  {
    "author": "emollick",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "Having done a lot of work on measuring performance, I don’t think this is very common. There are very few tasks that AI can do where there is not an upward trajectory. You can find tasks that no AI can do, or where there is saturation, but otherwise you get improvement over time.",
    "full_post": "Having done a lot of work on measuring performance, I don’t think this is very common. There are very few tasks that AI can do where there is not an upward trajectory. You can find tasks that no AI can do, or where there is saturation, but otherwise you get improvement over time.\n\nThere is a sort of file drawer bias: AI benchmarks that don’t meaningfully benchmark performance are dropped, but mostly because they are either 0 or 100. The whole point of benchmarks is to measure something about AI performance. Verisimilitude is a different matter, though.",
    "url": "https://x.com/emollick/status/2045638546397012174",
    "key_claims": ["AI performance improves over time on most tasks", "Benchmarks are dropped if not useful, not due to p-hacking"]
  },
  {
    "author": "emollick",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "Key to note that AI scientists are not experts on labor. Some other economists active on X doing work on AI & labor: @alexolegimas, @danielrock, @joshgans & @robseamans (among many others)\n\nBut worth noting that economists don’t have a consensus either: https://aleximas.substack.com/p/how-will-ai-driven-automation-actually",
    "full_post": "Key to note that AI scientists are not experts on labor. Some other economists active on X doing work on AI & labor: @alexolegimas, @danielrock, @joshgans & @robseamans (among many others)\n\nBut worth noting that economists don’t have a consensus either: https://aleximas.substack.com/p/how-will-ai-driven-automation-actually\n\nQuoted: Dario is wrong. He knows absolutely nothing about the effects of technological revolutions on the labor market.",
    "url": "https://x.com/emollick/status/2045616895592706291",
    "key_claims": ["AI scientists not labor experts", "Economists lack consensus on AI's labor impact"]
  },
  {
    "author": "emollick",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "One thing thing about AI, for better and worse, is that \"everything around me is somebody's life work\" is no longer a true assumption going forward.",
    "full_post": "One thing thing about AI, for better and worse, is that \"everything around me is somebody's life work\" is no longer a true assumption going forward.",
    "url": "https://x.com/emollick/status/2045318277958709540",
    "key_claims": ["AI changes assumption that creations require human life's work"]
  },
  {
    "author": "emollick",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "A major lesson to take away from Opus 4.7 is that, while there is a lot of arguments about implementation choices and personality, models keep improving measurably on economically important tasks with each release (it has been two months since Opus 4.6), with no signs of slowdown",
    "full_post": "A major lesson to take away from Opus 4.7 is that, while there is a lot of arguments about implementation choices and personality, models keep improving measurably on economically important tasks with each release (it has been two months since Opus 4.6), with no signs of slowdown",
    "url": "https://x.com/emollick/status/2045314251804324080",
    "key_claims": ["AI models continue improving on economic tasks", "No slowdown in progress"]
  },
  {
    "author": "garrytan",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "lmao strong “Dropbox is just rsync” vibes\n\nIf it were that easy then you would also have 8 figure contracts",
    "full_post": "lmao strong “Dropbox is just rsync” vibes\n\nIf it were that easy then you would also have 8 figure contracts\n\nReplied to: don't they make ai support agents? if yes then it's not that deep😭 package @livekit with stt, llm, tts model and deploy it on an ec2. add twilio sip trunking, function calling and observability.",
    "url": "https://x.com/garrytan/status/2045613850066817191",
    "key_claims": ["Building AI support agents is more complex than described", "Ease of building doesn't equate to commercial success"]
  },
  {
    "author": "swyx",
    "date_of_post": "2026-04-18",
    "text": "notes\n\nThe general loop is do good work and then tell people about it.  Example (4M views https://t.co/HONnO7qpCW - 1-2 months of work, 2 days of writing)\n\nSowing:\n- doing good work, even in a niche, is interesting to people not in the niche. (example: best pokemon player talking about his craft)\n- use [TOOL] for everything, solve and find all problems\n- talk to customers who were using [TOOL] inefficiently. realized that there were basic things they werent doing right, and it felt too basic to write but it reached a wide audience because he solved a pain point that customers had but employees maybe wouldnt experience\n\nReaping:\n1. Tell a story. (not simply \"how we built x\", \"how to do x\" - more informative/subtle than that)\n2. Keep it as simple as possible\n3. Don't sell something you don't believe in - if you dont use it, dont write about it.\n4. Share secrets - some \"real alpha\" that you're even a little scared to reveal to competitors\n\nmisc:\n- Q: How do you write? A: very unstructured... \"you have nothing until you have everything\", range from\n- Q: how much time do you spend writing for work? A: very spiky - one week a lot, one week just heads down engineering, aim for 50-50 sowing-reaping\n- Q: how do you make diagrams? A: Claude Design - make 5 different SVGs, make a concept and choose one. diagrams, code snippets. use principles of claude design, give design system and references.\n- Q: how much time you reading articles? too much marketing slop. \"i read our slack alot\". \n- Q: getting feedback before posting - use ABCD feedback framework.\n- Q: series - Lessons from Claude Code\n- make sales collateral\n- Q: did u make something more / less popular than expected? -> the skills post\n\nTLDR: Build implicit knowledge, and then make it explicit.",
    "full_post": "notes\n\nThe general loop is do good work and then tell people about it.  Example (4M views https://t.co/HONnO7qpCW - 1-2 months of work, 2 days of writing)\n\nSowing:\n- doing good work, even in a niche, is interesting to people not in the niche. (example: best pokemon player talking about his craft)\n- use [TOOL] for everything, solve and find all problems\n- talk to customers who were using [TOOL] inefficiently. realized that there were basic things they werent doing right, and it felt too basic to write but it reached a wide audience because he solved a pain point that customers had but employees maybe wouldnt experience\n\nReaping:\n1. Tell a story. (not simply \"how we built x\", \"how to do x\" - more informative/subtle than that)\n2. Keep it as simple as possible\n3. Don't sell something you don't believe in - if you dont use it, dont write about it.\n4. Share secrets - some \"real alpha\" that you're even a little scared to reveal to competitors\n\nmisc:\n- Q: How do you write? A: very unstructured... \"you have nothing until you have everything\", range from\n- Q: how much time do you spend writing for work? A: very spiky - one week a lot, one week just heads down engineering, aim for 50-50 sowing-reaping\n- Q: how do you make diagrams? A: Claude Design - make 5 different SVGs, make a concept and choose one. diagrams, code snippets. use principles of claude design, give design system and references.\n- Q: how much time you reading articles? too much marketing slop. \"i read our slack alot\". \n- Q: getting feedback before posting - use ABCD feedback framework.\n- Q: series - Lessons from Claude Code\n- make sales collateral\n- Q: did u make something more / less popular than expected? -> the skills post\n\nTLDR: Build implicit knowledge, and then make it explicit.\n\nQuoted: https://x.com/i/article/2027446899310313472",
    "url": "https://x.com/swyx/status/2045579294542622743",
    "key_claims": ["Do good work and communicate it effectively", "Use AI tools like Claude Design for diagrams", "Share genuine insights and secrets in writing"]
  }
]
